hero.title: PDF Converter
hero.tagline: Quickly convert PDF pages into high-quality JPG or PNG images. Choose the output format, preview the converted result, and download images for sharing, thumbnails, or embedding — no account required.

hint.text: Do you want to convert image type instead?

whatIs.heading: What is PDF conversion
whatIs.body: PDF-to-image conversion renders each page of the document as a raster image at a defined resolution. The output is a set of image files (one per page) that capture the visual appearance of the PDF — including text rendered as pixels, embedded images, and vector graphics rasterized at the specified DPI.

whenToConvert.heading: When to convert
whenToConvert.body: Converting PDF pages to images is appropriate when you need to embed document content in a context that doesn't support PDF rendering (for example, web pages that display static thumbnails, presentation slides, or messaging platforms that only accept images).

howBehaves.heading: How conversion behaves
howBehaves.body: A PDF-to-image converter renders each page at a fixed resolution and saves the result in the chosen format. The quality and file size of the output depend on the resolution (DPI) and the selected image format.

quality.heading: Quality and performance
quality.resolution: Resolution: Higher DPI produces sharper images but larger files.
quality.format: Format: JPEG uses lossy compression (smaller files, visible artifacts at low quality); PNG uses lossless compression (larger files, no artifacts, supports transparency).
quality.processing: Processing time: Each page requires independent rendering; multi-page PDFs take proportionally longer.

benefits.heading: Benefits of conversion
benefits.item1: Makes PDF content usable in image-only contexts.
benefits.item2: Allows individual pages to be shared, cropped, or annotated as images.
benefits.item3: Produces a visual representation that matches the exact PDF rendering.

privacy.heading: Privacy & retention
privacy.body: Files are processed on short-lived server infrastructure. Converted images are made available for download and then deleted automatically after a short retention window. Do not upload documents containing personal or sensitive information if you cannot accept the associated privacy risk.

practical.heading: Practical tips
practical.item1: For thumbnails or web previews, lower DPI (72–96) is usually sufficient.
practical.item2: For high-quality archival images, use higher DPI (150–300) and PNG.
practical.item3: For large PDFs, convert only the pages you actually need.

usefulWhen.heading: Useful when
usefulWhen.item1: You need to share individual pages as images.
usefulWhen.item2: You want to embed PDF pages in presentations or web content.
usefulWhen.item3: You need to extract visual content from a PDF.

faq.heading: FAQ
faq.q1: Q: Does the conversion preserve all visual content?
faq.a1: A: Yes — the converter renders the page at the specified DPI, capturing text, images, and graphics as they appear in the PDF.
faq.q2: Q: Can I convert a multi-page PDF?
faq.a2: A: Yes — each page is converted to a separate image file.
faq.q3: Q: Which format is better, JPG or PNG?
faq.a3: A: JPG for smaller files with minor quality loss; PNG for lossless quality with transparency support.
faq.q4: Q: Why is my output image blurry?
faq.a4: A: The DPI setting may be too low. Try a higher resolution setting.
faq.q5: Q: Can I convert password-protected PDFs?
faq.a5: A: Not directly — the PDF must be unlocked before conversion.

howItWorks.step1: Drag & drop your PDF or click Browse File to choose one. The file name and size are shown in the drop zone.
howItWorks.step2: Select the output format (JPG or PNG) using the Convert to selector.
howItWorks.step3: Tap Convert. A progress bar shows upload and conversion status.
howItWorks.step4: When ready, the converted image appears with a Download button.

note: Note: The converted file will be stored temporarily for a short time. Please download it within the available window.

guide.title: How to Convert PDF Files Without Breaking Layout (Smart & Simple Guide)
guide.intro: PDFs are great for sharing — but not always for editing. You've probably experienced this: you need to edit a PDF but it's locked, want to extract text but can't, or need a Word or Excel version but formatting breaks.
guide.cta: 👉 In this guide, you'll learn how to convert PDFs safely, preserve formatting, and choose the right format for your needs.

whatIs.heading: 📄 What Is a PDF Converter?
whatIs.body: A PDF converter is a tool that allows you to:
whatIs.note: Modern tools support both directions and multiple formats, making them highly flexible. Think of it as a bridge between formats.

whyConvert.heading: 🤔 Why Convert PDFs?
whyConvert.item1: ✏️ Edit Content Easily — convert to Word or Excel to modify text, update data, and reuse content.
whyConvert.item2: 📊 Extract Data — convert PDF → Excel to save time and avoid manual typing.
whyConvert.item3: 🖼 Use Content in Other Formats — convert to image to share visuals or embed in presentations.
whyConvert.item4: 📤 Improve Compatibility — some systems require specific file types; conversion solves this instantly.

howWorks.heading: ⚙️ How PDF Conversion Works
howWorks.body: When you convert a PDF, the system reads the file structure, identifies text, images, and layout, then rebuilds content in the new format. Good tools try to preserve layout and keep fonts and spacing intact.

whyBreaks.heading: 🧠 Why Formatting Sometimes Breaks
whyBreaks.body: PDFs are built for display, not structure. Text may not be stored logically and layout may be "visual only" — so converters sometimes have to guess structure. This is why results vary.

bestPractices.heading: ✅ Best Practices for Accurate Conversion
bestPractices.item1: 🔹 Use clean source files — clear text, standard fonts, simple layout.
bestPractices.item2: 🔹 Choose the right output format — Word for editing, Excel for tables, JPG for sharing visuals.
bestPractices.item3: 🔹 Check after conversion — always review alignment, fonts, and tables.
bestPractices.item4: 🔹 Avoid re-converting files — each step can introduce errors; always convert from the original PDF.
bestPractices.item5: 🔹 Use a reliable tool — a good converter should preserve formatting and support multiple formats.

stepByStep.heading: 🚀 Step-by-Step: Convert a PDF
stepByStep.note: Most tools complete this in seconds.

scenarios.heading: 🧩 Common Conversion Scenarios
scenarios.item1: 📄 PDF → Word — edit documents, update text
scenarios.item2: 📊 PDF → Excel — extract tables, work with data
scenarios.item3: 🖼 PDF → Image — share pages visually, use in presentations
scenarios.item4: 📥 Word/Image → PDF — create shareable documents, preserve formatting

proTips.heading: 💡 Pro Tips
proTips.item1: 📌 Convert a small test file first
proTips.item4: 📌 Keep a backup of the original file

faq.heading: ❓ FAQ
faq.q1: Does PDF conversion always keep formatting?
faq.a1: Not always — but good tools preserve most layout and structure.
faq.a2: Yes, but you may need OCR (text recognition) for accurate results.
faq.q3: What's the best format to convert to?
faq.a3: Editing → Word, data → Excel, sharing → PDF/Image.
faq.a4: Most tools process files securely and temporarily.

guide.conclusion: PDF conversion isn't just about changing formats — it's about unlocking your content. With the right approach, you can edit documents easily, extract useful data, and share content in any format.
guide.ctaBtn: 👉 Try it here: PDF Converter Tool
